item.title: Gilts: blame the hedge funds?
item.description: No one’s doing it like the Brits

item.title: Trump’s Iran playbook was written in the 1980s
item.description: From oil threats to ultimatums, the US president has a decades-old view of how to confront Tehran

item.title: America keeps bailing out Trump
item.description: The country’s underlying strengths are absorbing his erratic choices, including the war on Iran

item.title: The Shakespearean animus of Botín and Orcel
item.description: The Italian’s bid for Commerzbank may prove to be the final act in European banking’s rivalrous drama

item.title: Iran war is a risk to the flow of Gulf funds around the globe
item.description: World has come to depend on capital from the region more deeply than many realise

item.title: The ghost bankers haunting the City
item.description: Unable to leave the financial district, some senior staff appear to be working long after handing in their pass

item.title: ‘EU Inc’ is a missed opportunity for start-ups
item.description: Leaders who want more innovative European business must insist on a bolder approach

item.title: War with Iran has exposed American fragility at sea
item.description: The move from ‘efficiency’ to ‘resilience’ in maritime supply chains has a long way to go

item.title: How the Iran war could derail the AI boom
item.description: The entire chip supply chain depends on energy and chemical imports from the Middle East

item.title: Pakistan and Afghanistan tensions reach breaking point
item.description: However difficult it may be, the world needs to re-engage with the troubled region

item.title: Reading Socrates in Silicon Valley
item.description: Self-proclaimed stoics who denounce self-examination only prove the bankruptcy of the tech bro worldview

item.title: Is a plumbing career the future?
item.description: Skilled trades regarded as safe from automation but still face social resistance

item.title: Time to shear the UK housing market of the dreaded ‘fleecehold’
item.description: Estate management charges can leave buyers of new-built homes with unforeseen costs as they pay annually for maintenance

item.title: Chart of the Week: Global rates show deeper war fears
item.description: Investors are becoming increasingly hawkish

item.title: We should all be playing pothole politics
item.description: Living in the ‘Potswolds’ has made me one of the Angry Local People pointing at our damaged roads

item.title: Iran war raises the risk of a bond market shock
item.description: Wild swings in UK gilts point to strains that will hurt government finances and jack up borrowing costs for us all

item.title: Uber fights to stay in the robotaxi race
item.description: Ride-hailing company must make up lost ground on driverless vehicles, or risk being left in the rear-view mirror

item.title: History is tragically repeating itself in Lebanon
item.description: Ensuring the country’s survival — and Hizbollah’s weakening — is Trump’s best hope for Middle East success

item.title: Tokaj is so much more than a sweet wine
item.description: Hungary’s most famous wine region has made a huge success of dry whites

item.title: Aston Martin could do with a carpool companion
item.description: Brand still has global cachet but it is finding life tricky as a tiny luxury-car maker

item.title: Games we play as children echo through our adult lives
item.description: What can chess or hide-and-seek teach us about navigating relationships with others?

item.title: Britain must be more vigilant to the risk of sabotage by hostile states
item.description: If Moscow and Beijing are determined to destabilise the west then new strategies are needed

item.title: ‘Vladimir’ and the enduring appeal of campus satire
item.description: Weird rules, shifting cultural politics, contested parking spaces — universities still have all the ingredients for drama

item.title: The new old world
item.description: A Gulf war, an oil spike, a tense Europe: the 20th century is back

item.title: Restaurant Gordon Ramsay High, London: £776 of meh — review
item.description: The sweary chef thinks critics have got it in for him, but it’s his restaurant that is the problem
